summary: Tech-spec is well-structured and covers all major required sections. Architecture, decisions, wave organization, audit wave, and final wave are all present and correct. 14 tasks are within the limit. Three issues require fixes: (1) audit and QA tasks are missing required task fields, (2) reviewer skill 'test-reviewer' used in Tasks 3 and 8 does not match the catalog skill 'test-master', (3) a contradiction exists between user-spec NFR-3 (Firefox support) and tech-spec D2 (Firefox explicitly excluded) that should be acknowledged.

findings.description: Tasks 11 (Code Audit), 12 (Security Audit), 13 (Test Audit), and 14 (QA) are missing the required 'Files to modify' and 'Files to read' fields. All tasks in the spec must include these fields, even if the value is 'none' or a glob pattern like 'src/**'.
findings.fix: Add 'Files to modify: none' and 'Files to read: src/**' (or appropriate scope) to Tasks 11–14. For Task 14 (QA) also ensure Files to read references test files and user-spec acceptance criteria.

findings.description: Tasks 11, 12, and 13 (audit wave) have single-sentence descriptions. The spec requires 2–3 sentences explaining WHAT the task does (not HOW). Task 11 description is 'Holistic code quality review of all geo-fix source code.' — one sentence. Tasks 12 and 13 have two sentences but Task 11 has only one.
findings.fix: Expand Task 11 description to 2–3 sentences. Example addition: 'Review covers code structure, error handling, edge cases, and adherence to Python best practices. Output is a findings list with severity levels and recommended fixes.'

findings.description: Tasks 3 (mitmproxy addon) and 8 (main entry point) list 'test-reviewer' as a reviewer. The skill catalog uses 'test-master' (as confirmed by its use in Task 13). 'test-reviewer' does not appear to be a valid catalog entry.
findings.fix: Replace 'test-reviewer' with 'test-master' in the Reviewers field of Tasks 3 and 8.

findings.description: User-spec NFR-3 states the tool should work for 'any browser (Chrome, Edge, Firefox) and desktop applications using the system proxy.' Tech-spec Decision D2 explicitly excludes Firefox, citing CA store and proxy configuration complexity. This contradiction is unresolved in the spec.
findings.fix: Add a note in the Overview or in D2 acknowledging the NFR-3 conflict and stating that Firefox support is descoped per user agreement (as mentioned in D2 'User confirmed Chrome/Edge is sufficient'). Alternatively, update NFR-3 in the user-spec to reflect the agreed scope.

findings.description: Task 8 (main entry point) lists 'Files to read: all src/*.py' but is in the same wave (Wave 3) as Tasks 6 and 7, which create src/setup_wizard.py and src/tray.py. If Wave 3 tasks run in parallel, Task 8 cannot read files that Tasks 6 and 7 are simultaneously creating.
findings.fix: Move Task 8 (main entry point) to Wave 4, or split Wave 3 into two sub-waves: Wave 3a (Tasks 6 and 7) and Wave 3b (Task 8). This makes the dependency chain explicit.
